data.title: Bank OZK Q2 earnings beat, helped by growth in loans, deposits
data.description:
Bank OZK (NASDAQ:OZK) stock gained 1.1% in Thursday after-hours trading after the bank posted record earnings that exceeded the Wall Street consensus.

Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.58, topping the consensus estimate of $1.55, rose from $1.47 in Q1 and $1.52 in last year's Q2.

"Our excellent results for the quarter included record net income, record EPS, record net interest income, excellent growth in loans and deposits, and solid asset quality," said Chairman and CEO George Gleason.

"Our strong results for the first half of the year put us in a great position to achieve" its goal of improving on its record 2024 net income and EPS, management said. 

Provision for credit losses dropped to $35.2M from $38.4M in the prior quarter and $73.6M a year ago. 

Net interest income of $397M, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $388M, increased from $375M in Q1 and $388M in last year's Q2. Net interest margin of 4.36% improved from 4.31% in Q1 and fell from 4.68% in Q2 2024.

"We continue to expect to achieve record net interest income for the full year of 2025," the company said.

Noninterest income of $31.3M compared with $34.7M in the previous quarter and $28.8M a year ago.

Pretax preprovision net revenue of $274.9M increased from $262.3M in Q1 and fell from $279.3M in last year's Q2.

Noninterest expense of $153.2M increased from $147.0M in Q1 and $137.5M in Q2 2024.

Bank OZK's (NASDAQ:OZK) Real Estate Specialties Group ("RESG") loan originations totaled $1.51B, up from $1.21B in the previous quarter and down from $1.60B in the year-ago quarter. "Many sponsors remain appropriately cautious regarding new projects given the heightened level of macroeconomic uncertainty and the continued tight Fed monetary policy," the company said.

Loans grew to $33.0B at June 30, 2025, from $31.1B at March 31, 2025. Deposits increased to $33.5B from $31.9B.

Conference call on July 18 at 8:30 AM ET.

Earlier, Bank OZK GAAP EPS of $1.58 beats by $0.03, revenue of $428M beats by $4.75M

data.title: Interactive Brokers stock perks up after Q2 earnings beat on higher trading volumes
data.description:
Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) stock climbed 4.8% in Thursday after-hours trading after posting better-than-expected second-quarter headline results, bolstered by higher customer trading volume.

Q2 2025 adjusted EPS of $0.51, topping the $0.47 average analyst estimate, dropped from $1.88 in the prior quarter, but advanced from $0.41 a year ago.

Total net revenue of $1.48B in Q2, surpassing the $1.40B consensus, rose from $1.40B in Q1 and $1.23B in Q2 2024.

Total daily average revenue trades accelerated to 3.55M from 3.52M in Q1 and +49% from the year-earlier period.

Commission revenue was $516M, up from $514M in the previous quarter and +27% Y/Y. Customer trading volume in stocks, options and futures increased 31%, 24% and 18%, respectively, Y/Y.

Execution, clearing, and distribution fees of $116M in Q2 compared with $121M in Q1 and inched up 1% from last year's Q2.

Net interest income accelerated to $860M from $770M in Q1 and $792M a year before.

The number of customer accounts on Interactive Brokers (NASDAQ:IBKR) of 3.87M rose from 3.62M in Q1 and +32% from Q4 2024.

data.title: F.N.B. GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.02, revenue of $438.2M beats by $14.03M
data.description:
• F.N.B. press release (NYSE:FNB): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.36 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $438.2M (+8.5% Y/Y) beats by $14.03M.

data.title: Orion Properties gets a revised acquisition offer from Kawa Capital
data.description:
Orion Properties (NYSE:ONL) received a revised unsolicited, non-binding offer from Kawa Capital for the proposed acquisition of all the outstanding shares the latter does not already own in the office REIT.

Shares were +3.90% post market to $2.66.

Kawa Capital has offered to acquire the shares of common stock for a cash consideration of $2.75 per share.

The board of Orion Properties had unanimously rejected Kawa Capital's $2.50 per share offer on July 9.

The company said its board will carefully review and evaluate the revised indication of interest, and does not intend to comment further on the offer until the board has completed its review.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Netflix posts earnings beat, raises FY25 revenue guidance
Key takeaways from Netflix's Q2, with stock dipping
data.description:
Netflix stock (NASDAQ:NFLX) slipped 1% in immediate postmarket trading after the company's second-quarter earnings beat expectations on top and bottom lines and the firm raised full-year revenue expectations.

Revenues grew nearly 16% year-over-year to land at $11.08B, topping $11.06B expected. Operating income jumped to $3.78B from a year-ago $2.6B, and up from last quarter's $3.35B. Meanwhile, operating margin ticked up to 34.1% from a year-ago 27.2% and last quarter's 31.7%.

Net income jumped 46% year-over-year, to $3.13B.

With Netflix having stopped reporting subscriber growth, investor attention has naturally shifted to key financials, notably including cash flows. And a pivot to advertising-supported subscriptions and an ongoing move into live programming offers investors more color to seek out around earnings reports.

For the full year, it's raised revenue expectations to $44.8B-$45.2B (from $43.5B-$44.5B) and now expects currency-neutral operating margin of 29.5%, up from 29% (30% on an as-reported basis).

Cash flow

Net cash from operations rose year-over-year, to $2.42B, but dipped from Q1's $2.79B. Similarly, free cash flow rose to $2.27B from a year-ago $1.21B, but was down from last quarter's $2.66B.

"Our capital allocation approach is unchanged—we prioritize profitable growth by reinvesting in our business, maintaining ample liquidity and returning excess cash (beyond several billion dollars of minimum cash and any used for selective M&A) to shareholders through share repurchases," the company noted. 

It boosted full-year free cash flow expectations to $8B-$8.5B thanks to a corresponding increase in its forecasts for revenue and operating margin.

Gross debt at quarter-end was $14.5B, and cash and equivalents came to $8.2B. The company noted starting a commercial paper program in May, giving it the ability to issue short-term unsecured notes of up to $3B.

Viewing metrics

The company's content slate has been notably loaded toward the second half of 2025, but even so, viewers watched 95B hours in the first half (up 1% year-over-year).

"Our viewing is very broad and we’re not dependent on any one title to drive engagement—forinstance, even our biggest titles that have tens of millions of views account for less than 1% oftotal viewing on Netflix in the reporting period," Netflix said.

And live programming is expanding, including two marquee boxing matches in the third quarter and another NFL doubleheader on Christmas Day.

Monetization

"Response to our recent price adjustments, as measured by member acquisition, churn and plan mix, has been broadly in line with our expectations," the company said. 

Meanwhile, on ad-supported progress: "We continue to make progress building our ads business and still expect to roughly double ads revenue in 2025." Netflix's U.S. upfronts are nearly complete with the "vast majority" of deals with major agencies already closed.

Netflix has focused on building out more capabilities for advertisers, including the rollout of its own first-party ad-tech platform, the Netflix Ads Suite.

The company's usual live earnings executive interview will go live at 4:45 p.m. ET.

data.title: RE/MAX announces higher June home sales report
data.description:
• RE/MAX June 2025 marks 5 straight months of home sales gains across 50 metro areas, with inventory up 30% YoY and median prices hitting $440,000.

  • Sales activity in June edged up 1.3% from May 2025 and rose 5.7% compared to June of last year.

  • Source: Press release

data.title: Dollar General CFO resigns, takes job at Nordstrom
data.description:
• Dollar General (NYSE:DG) announced late Wednesday that CFO Kelly Dilts will step down on August 28 after two years in the role, having accepted the same position at Nordstrom.
  • The discount store chain has begun the search for a new chief financial officer, according to a  filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.
  • An interim CFO has not been named.
  • Shares closed lower for a third straight day, bringing the three-day total to -4.5%.

data.description:
• Bank OZK press release (NASDAQ:OZK): Q2 GAAP EPS of $1.58 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $428M (+2.7% Y/Y) beats by $4.75M.

data.title: Jabil authorizes $1B share repurchase
data.description:
• Jabil (NYSE:JBL) said on Thursday that its board has authorized a share repurchase program of up to $1B in common stock. 
  • The shares will be repurchased from time to time using various methods, including in the open market at the company’s discretion and subject to market conditions.
  • Since 2016, Jabil has returned approximately $7 billion to shareholders through both share repurchases and dividends. As part of this shareholder return framework, Jabil has repurchased approximately 110.2M shares at an average price of $59.46.
  • Jabil (NYSE:JBL) shares were up 1.94% in extended trading.
